properties.headline: Special Weather Statement issued July 20 at 10:41PM MDT by NWS Grand Junction CO
properties.description:
At 1041 PM MDT, Doppler radar was tracking a strong thunderstorm 11
miles northeast of Aneth, or 34 miles southeast of Blanding, moving
northwest at 30 mph.

HAZARD...Wind gusts up to 50 mph and pea size hail.

SOURCE...Radar indicated.

IMPACT...Gusty winds could knock down tree limbs and blow around
unsecured objects. Minor hail damage to vegetation is
possible.

Locations impacted include...
Aneth, Montezuma Creek, and Hovenweep National Monument.

This includes the following highways...
U.S. Highway 160 in Colorado between mile markers 1 and 4.
Utah 162 between mile markers 6 and 31.
Colorado 41 between mile markers 1 and 9.
properties.areaDesc: Four Corners/Upper Dolores River; Southeast Utah

properties.headline: Flash Flood Warning issued July 20 at 9:08PM MDT until July 21 at 12:00AM MDT by NWS Grand Junction CO
properties.description:
FFWGJT

The National Weather Service in Grand Junction has issued a

* Flash Flood Warning for...
The Snyder Burn Scar in...
West Central Mesa County in west central Colorado...
Eastern Grand County in east central Utah...

* Until midnight MDT tonight.

* At 908 PM MDT, Doppler radar indicated thunderstorms producing
heavy rain over the Snyder Burn Scar. Flash flooding is ongoing or
expected to begin shortly.

Excessive rainfall over the burn scar will result in debris flows.
The debris flow can consist of rock, mud, vegetation and other loose
materials.

HAZARD...Life threatening flash flooding. Thunderstorms
producing flash flooding in and around the Snyder Burn
Scar.

SOURCE...Radar.

IMPACT...Life threatening flash flooding of areas in and around
the Snyder Burn Scar.

* Some locations that will experience flash flooding include...
mainly rural areas of West Central Mesa and Eastern Grand Counties

properties.headline: Flood Advisory issued July 20 at 9:04PM MDT until July 21 at 12:00AM MDT by NWS Grand Junction CO
properties.description:
* WHAT...Flooding caused by excessive rainfall is expected.

* WHERE...A portion of west central Colorado, including the
following county, Mesa.

* WHEN...Until midnight MDT tonight.

* IMPACTS...Minor flooding in low-lying and poor drainage areas.

* ADDITIONAL DETAILS...
- At 902 PM MDT, Doppler radar indicated heavy rain due to
thunderstorms. Minor flooding is ongoing or expected to begin
shortly in the advisory area. Between 0.8 and 1.2 inches of
rain have fallen.
- Some locations that will experience flooding include...
Fruita and Redlands.
- http://www.weather.gov/safety/flood

properties.headline: Flash Flood Warning issued July 20 at 9:01PM MDT until July 21 at 12:00AM MDT by NWS Grand Junction CO
properties.description:
FFWGJT

The National Weather Service in Grand Junction has issued a

* Flash Flood Warning for...
Western Mesa County in west central Colorado...

* Until midnight MDT tonight.

* At 901 PM MDT, Doppler radar indicated thunderstorms producing
heavy rain across the warned area. Flash flooding is ongoing or
expected to begin shortly.

HAZARD...Life threatening flash flooding. Thunderstorms
producing flash flooding.

SOURCE...Radar.

IMPACT...Life threatening flash flooding of creeks and streams,
urban areas, highways, streets and underpasses.

* Some locations that will experience flash flooding include...
Gateway and John Brown Creek

properties.headline: Flash Flood Warning issued July 20 at 8:45PM MDT until July 20 at 11:45PM MDT by NWS Grand Junction CO
properties.description:
FFWGJT

The National Weather Service in Grand Junction has issued a

* Flash Flood Warning for...
The Deer Creek burn scar in...
Western Montrose County in west central Colorado...
Northeastern San Juan County in southeastern Utah...

* Until 1145 PM MDT.

* At 845 PM MDT, Doppler radar indicated thunderstorms producing
heavy rain near the Deer Creek Burn Scar. Between 0.5 and 1 inch
of rain has fallen. Flash flooding is ongoing or expected to begin
shortly.

Excessive rainfall over the burn scar will result in debris flows.
The debris flow can consist of rock, mud, vegetation and other loose
materials.

HAZARD...Life threatening flash flooding. Thunderstorms
producing flash flooding in and around the Deer Creek
Burn Scar.

SOURCE...Radar.

IMPACT...Life threatening flash flooding of areas in and around
the Deer Creek Burn Scar.

* Some locations that will experience flash flooding include...
mainly rural areas of Western Montrose and Northeastern San Juan
Counties

This includes the following streams and drainages...
Ice Lake Creek, La Sal Creek, and West Paradox Creek.
